description: Browser-based and cloud-hosted development environments for coding without local setup.

entries.name: GitHub Codespaces
entries.summary: Full VS Code-in-the-browser dev environments spun up directly from a GitHub repo, with devcontainer support.
entries.paidFrom: Pay-as-you-go beyond free quota, billed per core-hour.

entries.name: Gitpod
entries.summary: Ephemeral, automated cloud dev environments configured via a repo-committed config file.
entries.freeTier: 50 free hours/month on the free plan.
entries.paidFrom: Pro plan billed per usage beyond free hours.

entries.name: Replit
entries.summary: Browser-based IDE supporting 50+ languages with instant hosting, multiplayer editing, and an AI agent.
entries.freeTier: Free tier with limited compute and storage for public Repls.

entries.name: CodeSandbox
entries.summary: Instant web-based IDE and sandboxes for frontend prototyping, with live preview and npm support.
entries.freeTier: Free tier with limited private sandboxes and compute.

entries.name: StackBlitz
entries.summary: WebContainers-powered IDE that runs Node.js entirely in-browser for near-instant project boot times.
entries.freeTier: Free for public projects with core editing features.
entries.paidFrom: Pro plan from $20/month for private projects.

entries.name: Google Cloud Shell
entries.summary: Free browser-based shell and code editor with pre-installed gcloud tooling, for managing GCP resources.
entries.freeTier: Free with weekly usage hour quota, no separate billing.

entries.name: AWS Cloud9
entries.summary: Cloud IDE running on an EC2 instance or connected to existing infrastructure, integrated with AWS services.
entries.freeTier: No separate Cloud9 fee; billed only for underlying EC2 compute (eligible for AWS Free Tier EC2 hours).
